title: streptanthus glandulosus subsp. niger
intro: Streptanthus glandulosus subsp. niger, also known as the black rockcress, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mustard family (Brassicaceae) native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of black rockcress include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting potential antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable herbal remedy. Black rockcress can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its roots and leaves can be used in herbal preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
